from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural‑language sentence from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build entity relationship graph
    entity_to_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in entity_to_triples:
            entity_to_triples[t.subject] = []
        entity_to_triples[t.subject].append(t)
        if t.object not in entity_to_triples:
            entity_to_triples[t.object] = []
        entity_to_triples[t.object].append(t)

    # Find the main subject (entity that appears most as subject)
    subject_counts = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_counts[t.subject] = subject_counts.get(t.subject, 0) + 1

    if subject_counts:
        main_subject = max(subject_counts, key=subject_counts.get)
    else:
        main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Simple mapping from predicate to a readable phrase template.
    templates = {
        "numberOfMembers": "{subj} has {obj} members",
        "season": "{subj} played in the {obj} season",
        "league": "{subj} competes in the {obj} league",
        "manager": "{subj} is managed by {obj}",
        "ground": "{subj} plays at {obj}",
        "fullName": "{subj}'s full name is {obj}",
        "chairman": "{subj} is chaired by {obj}",
        "location": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "owner": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
        "nickname": "{subj} is nicknamed {obj}",
        "chairmanTitle": "{subj}'s chairman holds the title {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
        "leader": "{subj} is led by {obj}",
        "part": "{subj} includes {obj}",
        "mayor": "{subj} is governed by mayor {obj}",
        "champions": "{subj} champions are {obj}",
        "country": "{subj} is in {obj}",
        "club": "{subj} played for {obj}",
        "capital": "{subj}'s capital is {obj}",
        "currency": "{subj}'s currency is {obj}",
        "demonym": "People from {subj} are called {obj}",
        "language": "{subj} speaks {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "{subj}'s leader belongs to {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "{subj}'s official language is {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "{obj} is the birthplace of {subj}",
        "youthclub": "{subj} was in youth club {obj}",
        "operator": "{subj} is operated by {obj}",
        "tenant": "{subj} is tenant of {obj}",
        "city": "{subj} is in the city of {obj}",
        "state": "{subj} is in the state of {obj}",
    }

    # Predicates where the relationship direction matters (subject manages/works for object)
    person_club_predicates = {"club"}
    manager_predicates = {"manager"}

    # Check if subject appears to be a person (has name-like characteristics)
    def is_person(entity_name):
        # Common indicators of person names
        if any(name in entity_name.lower() for name in ["john", "jens", "peter", "michele", "antonis", "dan", "giorgos", "james"]):
            return True
        # If entity has multiple words and no special characters typical of organizations
        parts = entity_name.split()
        if len(parts) >= 2 and not any(x in entity_name for x in ["FC", "AFC", "AC", "Club", "Team", "League", "Série", "Premier"]):
            return True
        return False

    # Predicates where object is typically a location (can be referenced as "which is in X")
    location_predicates = {"country", "city", "state", "location", "ground"}

    # Predicates where relationship should be reversed when object is processed first
    reverse_predicates = {"birthPlace", "youthclub"}

    # Build connected sentence using relative clauses
    all_processed = set()
    sentence_parts = []
    processed_triple_indices = set()
    entity_occurrences = {}

    # Start with main subject
    current_entity = main_subject
    all_processed.add(current_entity)

    # Process all triples in a connected manner
    remaining = list(enumerate(triples))

    # First, get triples where subject is main subject
    main_triples = [(i, t) for i, t in remaining if t.subject == current_entity]
    for idx, t in main_triples:
        remaining.remove((idx, t))
        processed_triple_indices.add(idx)
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "{subj} {pred} {obj}")
        phrase = tmpl.format(subj=t.subject, pred=t.predicate, obj=t.object)
        sentence_parts.append(phrase)
        all_processed.add(t.object)

    # Then process remaining triples by connecting to already processed entities
    while remaining:
        found = False
        for idx, t in remaining:
            if t.subject in all_processed:
                remaining.remove((idx, t))
                processed_triple_indices.add(idx)
                # Handle special case where object is a club and subject is a person
                if t.predicate == "club" and is_person(t.subject):
                    phrase = f"{t.subject} played for {t.object}"
                elif t.predicate == "manager" and is_person(t.subject):
                    phrase = f"{t.subject} manages {t.object}"
                else:
                    tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "{subj} {pred} {obj}")
                    phrase = tmpl.format(subj=t.subject, pred=t.predicate, obj=t.object)
                sentence_parts.append(phrase)
                all_processed.add(t.object)
                found = True
                break
            elif t.object in all_processed:
                remaining.remove((idx, t))
                processed_triple_indices.add(idx)
                # Reverse the relationship for proper grammar
                if t.predicate == "birthPlace":
                    phrase = f"{t.object} is the birthplace of {t.subject}"
                elif t.predicate == "youthclub":
                    phrase = f"{t.object} was in youth club {t.subject}"
                elif t.predicate == "manager":
                    # If object is a club and subject is a person, object manages subject (wrong direction)
                    # Actually, if t.object is in all_processed and t.predicate is manager,
                    # it means we know about the club and now we're learning about its manager
                    phrase = f"{t.object} is managed by {t.subject}"
                elif t.predicate == "club" and is_person(t.object):
                    # Object is a person with a club relationship
                    phrase = f"{t.object} played for {t.subject}"
                else:
                    tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "{subj} {pred} {obj}")
                    phrase = tmpl.format(subj=t.object, pred=t.predicate, obj=t.subject)
                sentence_parts.append(phrase)
                all_processed.add(t.subject)
                found = True
                break

        if not found and remaining:
            # No more connections, add remaining
            for idx, t in remaining:
                tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "{subj} {pred} {obj}")
                phrase = tmpl.format(subj=t.subject, pred=t.predicate, obj=t.object)
                sentence_parts.append(phrase)
                all_processed.add(t.subject)
                all_processed.add(t.object)
            break

    # Build final sentence with proper connectors
    if len(sentence_parts) == 0:
        return ""

    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        return sentence_parts[0] + "."

    # Create a single complex sentence with relative clauses - avoid repeating subject names
    result = sentence_parts[0]

    # Track which entities have been mentioned to avoid repetition
    mentioned_entities = {main_subject}

    for i, part in enumerate(sentence_parts[1:], 1):
        first_word = part.split()[0] if part.split() else ""

        # If the clause starts with an already mentioned entity, use relative clause
        if first_word in mentioned_entities or first_word == main_subject:
            # Convert to relative clause to avoid subject repetition
            if " is " in part:
                clause = part.replace(" is ", " which is ", 1)
            elif " in " in part:
                clause = part.replace(" in ", " which is in ", 1)
            elif " has " in part:
                clause = part.replace(" has ", " which has ", 1)
            elif " competes" in part:
                clause = part.replace(" competes", " which competes", 1)
            elif " plays" in part:
                clause = part.replace(" plays", " which plays", 1)
            elif " managed" in part:
                clause = part.replace(" managed", " which is managed", 1)
            else:
                clause = " which " + part
            result += ", " + clause
        else:
            # Use "and" to connect independent clauses
            result += ", and " + part

        # Track entities mentioned in this clause
        mentioned_entities.add(first_word)

    return result + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
